intitule: Profile of an Inspiring African Figure

enonce.texte:
Read the following profile of Arthur Zang, the Cameroonian inventor of the Cardio Pad, and answer the questions below.

Arthur Zang: A Cameroonian Innovator

Arthur Zang is a young Cameroonian engineer who invented the Cardio Pad, a medical device that allows heart examinations to be performed remotely. Born in 1986 in Yaoundé, he studied computer engineering at the University of Yaoundé I.

The idea for the Cardio Pad came to him when he realised that many people in rural areas did not have access to cardiologists. He wanted to create a solution that would bring medical expertise to these communities.

In 2013, Zang presented his invention at the Africa Innovation Summit in Cape Town, where he won the Royal Academy of Engineering's Africa Prize for Engineering Innovation. This award gave him the resources to develop and distribute his device.

Today, the Cardio Pad is used in several African countries. It has helped thousands of patients receive heart examinations and diagnoses without travelling long distances. Arthur Zang's work has shown that young Africans can create innovative solutions to solve local problems.

Despite the challenges he faced, including lack of funding and limited resources, Zang never gave up. He believes that perseverance and a clear vision are the keys to success. He hopes to inspire other young Africans to pursue careers in science and technology.
images: Arthur Zang holding the Cardio Pad, a medical tablet device for heart examinations. He is smiling and wearing a professional outfit.
The Cardio Pad being used by a nurse to examine a patient in a rural clinic in Africa.

questions.texte: Who is Arthur Zang and what is his main achievement?
questions.reponse: Arthur Zang is a Cameroonian engineer who invented the Cardio Pad, a medical device for remote heart examinations.

questions.texte: Analyse the motivation behind Arthur Zang's invention of the Cardio Pad.
questions.reponse: Arthur Zang was motivated by the lack of access to cardiologists in rural areas. He wanted to bring medical expertise to communities that were far from urban health centers.

questions.texte: Evaluate the impact of Arthur Zang's invention on healthcare in Africa.
questions.reponse: The Cardio Pad has had a significant impact by enabling remote heart examinations, reducing the need for long-distance travel, and making healthcare more accessible to rural communities. It demonstrates that African innovation can solve local health challenges.

sousQuestions.texte: List two benefits of the Cardio Pad mentioned in the text.
sousQuestions.reponse: Two benefits are: 1) It allows heart examinations to be performed remotely. 2) It has helped thousands of patients receive diagnoses without travelling long distances.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain how the Cardio Pad addresses a specific challenge in African healthcare.
sousQuestions.reponse: The Cardio Pad addresses the challenge of limited access to medical specialists in rural areas. It connects patients with cardiologists remotely, overcoming geographical barriers.

questions.texte: Identify the qualities that make Arthur Zang an inspiring figure according to the text.
questions.reponse: According to the text, Arthur Zang is inspiring because of his creativity, perseverance, vision, and determination to solve local problems despite challenges.

sousQuestions.texte: What challenges did Arthur Zang face in developing his invention?
sousQuestions.reponse: He faced challenges including lack of funding and limited resources.

sousQuestions.texte: What message does Arthur Zang hope to convey to other young Africans?
sousQuestions.reponse: He hopes to inspire other young Africans to pursue careers in science and technology and to believe in their ability to create innovative solutions.

intitule: Writing a Profile of an Inspiring Figure

enonce.texte:
You are asked to write a profile of an inspiring contemporary African personality. This could be an innovator, artist, leader, activist, or any figure who has made a positive impact.

Use the following structure to organize your profile:

  • Introduction: Introduce the person and explain why they are inspiring.
  • Background: Provide key information about their life, education, or early career.
  • Achievements: Describe their main accomplishments and contributions.
  • Impact: Explain the impact of their work on society or a specific community.
  • Conclusion: Summarize their legacy and what can be learned from them.

questions.texte: Write a profile of an inspiring African personality. Your profile should be between 200 and 250 words.
questions.reponse:
Example Profile: Wangari Maathai - The Tree Lady of Africa

Wangari Maathai is one of Africa's most inspiring figures. She was a Kenyan environmental activist and the first African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize in 2004. Her life's work shows that one person can make a difference.

Born in 1940 in rural Kenya, Maathai studied biology in the United States and later became a professor of veterinary anatomy at the University of Nairobi. In 1977, she founded the Green Belt Movement, an organization that encouraged women to plant trees to combat deforestation, soil erosion, and environmental degradation.

Through her movement, she planted over 51 million trees across Africa. Her work not only restored the environment but also empowered women, provided jobs, and improved food security. She believed that environmental conservation and human rights were connected, and she fought for democracy in Kenya.

Wangari Maathai's impact is undeniable. She inspired millions to care for the environment and to take action. Her message was simple: every person has a role to play in making the world a better place. She showed that grassroots movements can create real change.

Today, her legacy continues through the Green Belt Movement and the countless people she inspired. She reminds us that courage, determination, and a love for the Earth are essential qualities for leadership.

sousQuestions.texte: Write a strong introduction for your profile.
sousQuestions.reponse: An example introduction: 'Wangari Maathai is one of Africa's most inspiring figures. She was a Kenyan environmental activist and the first African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize in 2004. Her life's work shows that one person can make a difference.'

sousQuestions.texte: Describe the main achievements of the person you are profiling.
sousQuestions.reponse: An example: 'She founded the Green Belt Movement and planted over 51 million trees across Africa. Her work restored the environment, empowered women, provided jobs, and improved food security.'

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the impact of this person's work and write a conclusion.
sousQuestions.reponse: An example: 'Wangari Maathai's impact is undeniable. She inspired millions to care for the environment. Her message was simple: every person has a role to play. She showed that grassroots movements can create real change. Today, her legacy continues through the Green Belt Movement.'

intitule: Analysing the Qualities of an Inspiring Figure

enonce.texte:
Read the following quotes from famous African figures and answer the questions.

Quote 1: "The environment is the foundation of our lives. When we destroy it, we destroy ourselves." - Wangari Maathai

Quote 2: "It is a duty of the young to challenge the old." - Nelson Mandela

Quote 3: "I can change the world if I change my own world." - Arthur Zang

Quote 4: "Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world." - Nelson Mandela

questions.texte: Analyse the message conveyed by each quote and explain what it reveals about the speaker's values.
questions.reponse:
Quote 1 (Wangari Maathai): This quote reveals her commitment to environmental conservation. She believed that humans depend on the environment for survival and that destroying it is self-destructive.

Quote 2 (Nelson Mandela): This quote reflects his belief in progress and the importance of youth in challenging established ideas. It suggests that change comes from questioning the status quo.

Quote 3 (Arthur Zang): This quote shows his belief in personal responsibility. He thought that change begins with oneself and that individual actions can have a larger impact.

Quote 4 (Nelson Mandela): This quote highlights his belief in the power of education. He saw education as a tool for liberation and transformation, capable of creating a better world.

sousQuestions.texte: What does Wangari Maathai's quote reveal about her values?
sousQuestions.reponse: It reveals her deep commitment to environmental conservation and her belief that humans have a responsibility to protect nature. She viewed environmental destruction as a threat to humanity itself.

sousQuestions.texte: According to Nelson Mandela's quotes, what are two key values he promoted?
sousQuestions.reponse: Two key values he promoted are: 1) The importance of youth and their role in challenging old ideas. 2) The power of education to change the world.

questions.texte: Which of these quotes inspires you the most? Justify your choice with reasons.
questions.reponse: This answer is personal and depends on the student's perspective. A sample answer: 'I find Nelson Mandela's quote about education most inspiring because it shows that learning is not just for personal gain but for making the world a better place. It gives young people a sense of purpose and responsibility.'

enonce.texte:
Below are three inspiring African figures from different fields. Match each figure with their main achievement and the impact of their work.

FigureAchievementImpact
1. Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o
(Author)		A. Invented the Cardio PadD. Advanced African healthcare by enabling remote diagnoses
2. Arthur Zang
(Engineer)		B. Founded the Green Belt MovementE. Gave voice to African literature and cultural identity
3. Wangari Maathai
(Environmentalist)		C. Wrote novels promoting African cultureF. Planted millions of trees and empowered women

questions.texte: Match each figure with the correct achievement and impact.
questions.reponse:
Ngũgĩ wa Thiong'o: 1-C-E (He wrote novels promoting African culture and gave voice to African literature and cultural identity).

Arthur Zang: 2-A-D (He invented the Cardio Pad and advanced African healthcare by enabling remote diagnoses).

Wangari Maathai: 3-B-F (She founded the Green Belt Movement and planted millions of trees while empowering women).

questions.texte: Which of these three figures do you find most inspiring? Explain why.
questions.reponse: This answer is personal. A sample answer: 'I find Wangari Maathai most inspiring because she addressed environmental issues while also empowering women. Her work had a direct and visible impact on her community, showing how social and environmental activism can go hand in hand.'

intitule: Writing a Profile: Structure and Coherence

enonce.texte:
You have been asked to write a profile of an inspiring figure for a school magazine. Below is a list of sentences that are out of order. Rearrange them to create a coherent profile with a clear structure: Introduction, Background, Achievements, Impact, and Conclusion.

Sentences:

  • A. Through her movement, she planted over 51 million trees across Africa.
  • B. Wangari Maathai was a Kenyan environmental activist and the first African woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize.
  • C. Today, her legacy continues through the Green Belt Movement and the countless people she inspired.
  • D. Born in 1940, she studied biology in the United States and became a professor of veterinary anatomy at the University of Nairobi.
  • E. Her work not only restored the environment but also empowered women and improved food security.
  • F. She founded the Green Belt Movement in 1977 to combat deforestation and environmental degradation.
  • G. She shows that one person can make a difference.
  • H. Wangari Maathai is one of Africa's most inspiring figures.
  • I. Her message was simple: every person has a role to play.

questions.texte: Rearrange the sentences to form a coherent profile of Wangari Maathai. The profile should have a clear introduction, background, achievements, impact, and conclusion.
questions.reponse:
Correct Order:

Introduction: H, G

Background: D

Achievements: F, A

Impact: E, I

Conclusion: C

Full profile:

Wangari Maathai is one of Africa's most inspiring figures. She shows that one person can make a difference. Born in 1940, she studied biology in the United States and became a professor of veterinary anatomy at the University of Nairobi. She founded the Green Belt Movement in 1977 to combat deforestation and environmental degradation. Through her movement, she planted over 51 million trees across Africa. Her work not only restored the environment but also empowered women and improved food security. Her message was simple: every person has a role to play. Today, her legacy continues through the Green Belt Movement and the countless people she inspired.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the sentences that belong to the introduction.
sousQuestions.reponse: The introduction sentences are H and G.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the sentences that describe the impact of Wangari Maathai's work.
sousQuestions.reponse: The impact sentences are E and I.
